google.best_for: Photorealistic product shots and hero videos. Imagen 4.0 produces premium quality with excellent leather texture rendering. Veo 2.0 generates usable hero video loops from still images in ~33s at web-friendly file sizes.
google.weaknesses: No explicit style reference API for image sets — coherence relies on prompt consistency. Warm gold lighting in prompts produces consistent results but not guaranteed across different product types.

leonardo.custom_model_training_cost: Varies by plan — API plan includes training tokens
leonardo.best_for: Volume generation with 47 model choices. Good for variety and experimentation. Custom model training could solve the coherence problem long-term.
leonardo.weaknesses: Phoenix 1.0 shoe images have a more CGI/rendered look vs Imagen's photorealism. Oxford came out dark navy instead of brown leather. Loafer showed two shoes instead of one. Coherence between images is noticeably lower — different angles, compositions, color palettes.

adobe_firefly.note: Fritz will complete manual web test separately using the checklist at tests/media/adobe/firefly-web-test-checklist.md

comparison.image_set_coherence_winner: Google Imagen 4.0 (via prompt consistency) — Leonardo had more variation in color/angle/composition
comparison.video_generation_winner: Google Veo 2.0 (only provider with API video)
comparison.easiest_api: Google (Python SDK, 3-line generation) vs Leonardo (REST + polling)
comparison.most_model_variety: Leonardo (47 models including FLUX, Kino, custom training)
comparison.recommendation_for_pipeline: Google Imagen 4.0 as primary, Leonardo as secondary for variety/custom models, Adobe Firefly for manual style reference work
